explanations.9: The left number (8080) is the port on your computer; the right number (80) is the port inside the container—traffic from your browser on 8080 gets forwarded to the app running on 80 inside.
explanations.13: Even though this is a Joomla container, it's being set to use PostgreSQL (postgres) as the database instead of the default MySQL, so the username and password must match what the database container expects.
explanations.18: This tells Joomla to connect using the PostgreSQL database driver (pgsql) rather than MySQL, which matches the postgres:16.0-alpine image being used for the database.
explanations.24: Alpine is a super lightweight Linux distribution that keeps the Docker image small; postgres:16.0-alpine is PostgreSQL version 16.0 running on Alpine Linux.
explanations.29: This creates a PostgreSQL database named 'joomla_db', which must match the JOOMLA_DB_NAME value on line 16 so Joomla can find its database.
